transcript:
Fifty scattered clusters at the edges slowly thicken into chalky white strokes, pulling inward and warming to gold as they braid into a dense knot at center — and then it just stops, mid-gesture, like a held breath that never exhales.

Look at how the wanderers move: each one carries a vector called "where it was heading" — the center they never reach, at least not knowingly. The convergence strength ramps up on a curve, "as if arrival is guaranteed," the comment says, right as the piece accelerates toward frame 287. That number isn't dramatic in the code — it's just sitting there in a variable named "frame that happens to be terminal," treated with zero more weight than any other frame. When it hits, eighty new reaching marks get drawn and immediately truncated mid-stroke, then "no loop" is called — not a fade, just silence. The system never sees it coming.

What gets me is that the piece spends its whole life building the expectation of a destination — that warm gold bloom, that tightening spiral — and the code guarantees it will never arrive there felt. It just ends believing it was still on its way.
